Title: Bacillus amyloliquefaciens promotes cluster root formation of white lupin under low phosphorus by mediating auxin levels

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in roots after SQR9 inoculation under low phosphorus
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots under low phosphorus; induced by SQR9 inoculation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin distribution required to promote cluster root formation and phosphorus acquisition under low phosphorus
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LaPIN2-mediated auxin transport is essential for cluster root formation and for integrating plant- and bacterium-derived auxin pools under low phosphorus
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; VIGS knockdown; cluster root phenotyping with and without SQR9 inoculation; pharmacological inhibition by NPA supports transport requirement
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low phosphorus; SQR9 inoculation; NPA treatment
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome analysis from White Lupin Genome Portal / Hufnagel et al. (2020); RNA-seq reanalysis, accession NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: LaPIN2 knockdown strongly reduces cluster root formation; broader tradeoffs not reported
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV-based VIGS system used for knockdown; native promoter not characterized
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for improving phosphorus deficiency-adaptive root architecture, but functional auxin transport must be maintained for microbial auxin benefits to manifest
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaPIN2 expression is induced by SQR9 under low phosphorus. 2. LaPIN2 knockdown drastically reduces cluster root formation. 3. SQR9 cannot rescue the LaPIN2 knockdown phenotype, indicating LaPIN2 is essential for transporting pooled plant- and bacteria-derived auxin.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because it functions primarily as an auxin transporter rather than enzyme or transcription factor.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in bacterial auxin biosynthesis leading to IAA production; exact reaction step not specified in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cluster root formation and thereby improves phosphorus acquisition/use efficiency under low phosphorus
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Key biosynthetic step in bacterial IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by mutant analysis, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Bacterial ysnE-dependent auxin production is necessary for SQR9-induced cluster root formation in white lupin under low phosphorus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ysnE knockout mutant (ΔysnE); HPLC measurement of bacterial IAA; inoculation phenotype comparison; root metabolite/phenotype profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ΔysnE reduced root fresh weight promotion compared with wild-type SQR9 under normal and low phosphorus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful microbial target for bioinoculant engineering to enhance root architectural adaptation and phosphorus use efficiency under phosphorus deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SQR9 promotes white lupin cluster root formation under low phosphorus. 2. ysnE-deficient SQR9 produced much less IAA and failed to effectively induce cluster roots. 3. SQR9-derived auxin acts together with plant-derived auxin to promote cluster root formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states ysnE is associated with auxin biosynthesis in SQR9; exact enzymatic chemistry is not detailed. Source species differs from applied species and should not be merged.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: YUCCA family flavin monooxygenase-like auxin biosynthesis enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a YUCCA-mediated step in plant auxin biosynthesis leading to IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in cluster roots versus lateral roots without cluster roots; induced by SQR9 under low phosphorus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in roots/cluster roots under low phosphorus and after SQR9 inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cluster root formation and supports phosphorus acquisition under low phosphorus
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: White lupin roots, especially cluster-root-forming root tissue
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for plant-derived auxin contribution to cluster root formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LaYUC4 promotes plant auxin biosynthesis required for full cluster root formation under low phosphorus; knockdown reduces cluster roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-supported expression analysis; RT-qPCR; VIGS knockdown; cluster root phenotyping with and without SQR9 inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome analysis from White Lupin Genome Portal / Hufnagel et al. (2020); RNA-seq reanalysis, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown reduces cluster root formation; no broader agronomic penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to enhance low-phosphorus adaptive root traits, potentially in combination with microbial inoculants and auxin transport competence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaYUC4 is the most strongly implicated YUCCA gene in SQR9-induced cluster root formation. 2. LaYUC4 expression is induced by SQR9 and is high in cluster roots. 3. LaYUC4 knockdown reduces cluster root formation, but SQR9 can partially compensate through bacterial auxin supply.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper also mentions LaYUC7 and LaYUC9 as upregulated in cluster roots, but only LaYUC4 was functionally validated and is therefore a core gene.
